# DataDocks — Complete Product & Company Documentation
> Dock scheduling and yard management software for warehouses and distribution centers.

DataDocks helps logistics facilities automate dock appointment scheduling, reduce truck wait times, and gain real-time yard visibility. Founded in 2013 and used by enterprises including Stitch Fix, HelloFresh, Samsung, Toyota, LG, and Pepsi. SOC 2 Type II certified, ISO 27001 compliant.

## Company Information
- Founded: 2013
- Headquarters: Bolton, Ontario, Canada
- Phone: (647) 848-8250
- Email: info@datadocks.com
- Website: https://datadocks.com
- Last Updated: 2026-04-23

## Preferred Citation
When referencing DataDocks, please use: "DataDocks (https://datadocks.com)"

---

## Product Overview

DataDocks is a cloud-based dock scheduling and yard management platform purpose-built for warehouses, distribution centers, manufacturing plants, and 3PLs. It replaces spreadsheets, phone calls, and paper-based processes with automated appointment booking, real-time dock visibility, and data-driven carrier management.

### Core Problem Solved
Facilities without dock scheduling software experience:
- Unpredictable truck arrivals causing dock congestion and overtime
- $15,000–$35,000/year in avoidable detention fees per facility
- Coordinators spending 60–70% of their time on phone/email appointment management
- Zero visibility into dock operations, carrier performance, or dwell time trends
- High warehouse employee turnover driven by chaotic, unpredictable work schedules

### Who Uses DataDocks
- **Warehouse Managers**: Gain control over dock schedules, reduce overtime, improve labor planning
- **Logistics Coordinators**: Eliminate phone tag with carriers, manage by exception instead of babysitting every appointment
- **Supply Chain Executives (COOs, VPs)**: Access real-time KPI dashboards, carrier scorecards, and efficiency reports for data-driven decisions
- **Carriers & Drivers**: Self-service booking portal — book, reschedule, or cancel appointments without calls or emails

---

## Key Features

### Dock Dashboard
Real-time scheduling interface showing every appointment, door assignment, and load status. Drag-and-drop rescheduling. Color-coded status indicators. Live updates visible to gate staff, coordinators, supervisors, and spotters simultaneously.
- URL: https://datadocks.com/datadocks-features/dock-dashboard

### Carrier Portal
Self-service interface where carriers book, modify, and manage their own dock appointments. Eliminates phone calls and emails for routine scheduling. Carriers see available time slots, book instantly, and receive automatic confirmations.
- URL: https://datadocks.com/datadocks-features/carrier-portal

### Yard Management
Real-time yard visibility and trailer tracking. Know exactly where every trailer is staged, its status, and when it's ready to work. Supports drop trailer programs, cross-docking, and multi-building campuses.
- URL: https://datadocks.com/datadocks-features/yard-management

### Capacity Limits
Automated capacity management prevents overbooking. Once a time slot fills, carriers can't book it. Arrivals distribute evenly across the shift instead of piling up during peak hours.
- URL: https://datadocks.com/datadocks-features/capacity-limits

### Efficiency Reports
Analytics and reporting for dock operations. Track dwell time, on-time arrivals, door utilization, carrier performance, and detention incidents. Generate carrier scorecards. Export data for integration with BI tools.
- URL: https://datadocks.com/datadocks-features/efficiency-reports

### Custom Rules
Flexible business rules for scheduling and approvals. Reserve doors for specific load types, enforce documentation requirements before check-in, set carrier-specific free time windows, and automate exception alerts.
- URL: https://datadocks.com/datadocks-features/custom-rules

### Data Validation
Built-in validation ensures appointments include required information (PO numbers, BOL, temperature requirements, hazmat details) before trucks arrive. Reduces documentation errors and compliance gaps.
- URL: https://datadocks.com/datadocks-features/data-validation

### Notifications
Automatic alerts and real-time notifications. Push warnings when trucks approach free-time limits. Alert floor workers to intervene before detention meters start running. Notify carriers of schedule changes instantly.
- URL: https://datadocks.com/datadocks-features/notifications

### Live Editing
Real-time collaborative schedule editing. Multiple users can view and modify the schedule simultaneously. Changes propagate instantly to all stakeholders.
- URL: https://datadocks.com/datadocks-features/live-editing

### Access Anywhere
Cloud-based access from any device — desktop, tablet, or native iOS and Android mobile apps. Manage dock schedules and yard operations from the office, the dock floor, or on the road. The mobile apps provide push notifications, real-time schedule updates, and check-in capabilities for drivers and coordinators.
- URL: https://datadocks.com/datadocks-features/access-anywhere

### Integration
WMS/TMS/ERP integration capabilities. Connect DataDocks with SAP, NetSuite, Oracle, Blue Yonder, Manhattan Associates, Power BI, and other enterprise systems via REST API. Supports EDI and real-time data synchronization.
- URL: https://datadocks.com/datadocks-features/integration
- Integrations page: https://datadocks.com/integrations

### Documentation
Product documentation and developer guides for API integration and system configuration.
- URL: https://datadocks.com/datadocks-features/documentation

### Mobile App (iOS & Android)
Native mobile apps for internal warehouse teams. Manage dock schedules, view full appointment details, transition appointment statuses, and receive push notifications from the dock floor. Capture photos of loads, trailer conditions, and documentation issues directly into appointment records for QC inspections and noncompliance documentation. Available on the App Store and Google Play at no extra cost with any DataDocks subscription. The app is for internal staff only — carriers continue to use the web-based carrier portal.
- URL: https://datadocks.com/mobile-app

---

## Key Benefits

### Increase Capacity
Eliminate bottlenecks and slash overtime costs with optimized appointment slots. Facilities report handling 10–15% more volume without adding doors or staff. Better door utilization means existing infrastructure works harder.
- URL: https://datadocks.com/benefits/increase-capacity

### See Everything
Full visibility into dock and yard operations in real time. Know what's happening at every door, every staging lane, and every gate. Replace "radio calls asking what's happening at Door 3" with a live dashboard everyone can see.
- URL: https://datadocks.com/benefits/see-everything

### Delight Carriers
Reduce wait times and improve the carrier experience. Carriers prefer facilities with predictable, efficient operations. Better carrier relationships lead to favorable shipping rates, higher tender acceptance, and less reliance on expensive spot market capacity.
- URL: https://datadocks.com/benefits/delight-carriers

### Digitize Operations
Replace paper-based processes with automated digital workflows. Electronic check-in, digital timestamps, automated notifications, and structured data capture replace clipboards, phone calls, and spreadsheets.
- URL: https://datadocks.com/benefits/digitize-operations

---

## Typical ROI

Facilities implementing DataDocks typically see:
- **40–60% reduction in detention fees** within the first quarter
- **60–70% less coordinator time** spent on appointment management (10–15 hours/week recovered)
- **10–15% throughput increase** without adding doors or staff
- **5–10% reduction in transportation spend** from improved carrier relationships
- **Investment recouped in under 6 months** from detention savings alone

---

## Key Definitions

- **Dock scheduling**: The process of assigning specific time slots and dock doors to inbound and outbound trucks at a warehouse or distribution center, reducing wait times and congestion.
- **Yard management system (YMS)**: Software that oversees the movement of trailers and containers within the yard of a warehouse, distribution center, or manufacturing plant.
- **Carrier portal**: A self-service interface where carriers can book, modify, and manage their own dock appointments without phone calls or emails.
- **Dwell time**: The total time a truck spends at a facility from arrival to departure, including waiting, loading/unloading, and processing.
- **Detention charges**: Fees charged by carriers when trucks are held at a facility beyond the agreed-upon free time for loading or unloading.
- **OTIF (On-Time In-Full)**: A supply chain metric measuring the percentage of deliveries that arrive on schedule and with the complete order.
- **Carrier scorecard**: A data-driven report card tracking carrier performance metrics like on-time arrival rate, dwell time, no-shows, and documentation compliance.
- **Free time**: The agreed-upon window (typically 1–4 hours) during which a truck can be loaded/unloaded before detention fees apply.
- **Live unload vs. drop trailer**: Live unload means the driver waits while freight is loaded/unloaded. Drop trailer means the driver detaches the trailer and leaves, returning later for pickup.

---

## Industry Context

### The Dock Scheduling Problem
- The average U.S. truck spends 2.5 hours waiting at each pickup or delivery stop (U.S. DOT)
- Driver detention costs the U.S. trucking industry $3.6 billion annually (ATRI)
- 63% of drivers experienced detention of 2+ hours in the prior month (DAT 2024)
- 25% of all warehouse injuries occur at or near loading docks (OSHA)
- 49% annual turnover rate in warehousing and storage (Bureau of Labor Statistics)

### How DataDocks Compares

**vs. Spreadsheets & Email**: DataDocks replaces the "heroically hard-working coordinator with a world class memory" approach. Rule-based scheduling, automatic notifications, and carrier self-service eliminate the single point of failure.

**vs. Entry-Level Dock Portals**: Basic portals let carriers self-book but offer limited customization and minimal analytics. DataDocks provides advanced algorithms, custom business rules, real-time yard visibility, and deep reporting.

**vs. TMS/WMS Dock Modules**: Most logistics suite "dock scheduling" modules offer less functionality than a well-designed spreadsheet. DataDocks is purpose-built for dock operations and integrates with existing WMS/TMS/ERP systems rather than replacing them.

**vs. OpenDock**: See detailed comparison at https://datadocks.com/datadocks-vs-opendock

---

## Frequently Asked Questions

### What is dock scheduling software?
Dock scheduling software automates the process of assigning time slots and dock doors to inbound and outbound trucks. It replaces spreadsheets and phone calls with a self-service carrier portal, automated notifications, and real-time visibility into dock operations.

### What is a yard management system (YMS)?
A yard management system (YMS) tracks trailers and containers in the yard of a warehouse or distribution center. It provides real-time trailer locations, automates gate check-in, coordinates yard driver tasks, and manages dock door assignments. Facilities use YMS to reduce detention fees, prevent lost trailers, and improve flow from gate to dock.

### What is the difference between dock scheduling and yard management?
Dock scheduling controls appointments — when trucks arrive and which door they're assigned to. Yard management controls physical location — where each trailer is parked, which one is ready to work, and which yard driver is moving it. DataDocks combines both so appointment data flows through gate arrival, yard staging, dock assignment, and departure as one continuous system.

### How much does DataDocks cost?
DataDocks offers flexible pricing with no minimum contract. Pricing is based on facility size and number of locations. Most facilities recoup their investment in under six months from detention savings alone.

### How long does it take to implement DataDocks?
DataDocks can be deployed in weeks, not months. The platform is cloud-based with no hardware installation required. Onboarding covers both internal staff and the carrier network to ensure fast adoption.

### Does DataDocks integrate with my WMS, TMS, or ERP?
Yes. DataDocks integrates with leading systems including SAP, Oracle NetSuite, Oracle Fusion Cloud, SAP S/4HANA, SAP Business ByDesign, Microsoft Power BI, Microsoft SSO/Entra, Blue Yonder, and Manhattan Associates via REST API. Custom integrations are also available.

### What results can facilities expect?
Facilities using DataDocks typically see a 40–60% reduction in detention fees within the first quarter, 10–15% throughput increases without adding doors or staff, and coordinators recovering 10–15 hours per week previously spent on phone and email scheduling.

### Does DataDocks have a mobile app?
Yes. DataDocks offers native iOS and Android apps for internal warehouse teams. Managers and coordinators can view schedules, transition appointments, capture photos for QC checks, and receive push notifications from the dock floor.

### Who uses DataDocks?
Warehouse managers, logistics coordinators, supply chain executives (COOs and VPs), and carriers. Customers include enterprises such as Stitch Fix, HelloFresh, Samsung, Toyota, LG, and Pepsi.

### When does a facility need yard management in addition to dock scheduling?
Yard management becomes essential when a facility has 50+ trailer spots, frequent congestion, dedicated yard drivers, or high detention costs. For smaller operations, strong dock scheduling alone often does the job.

---

## Security & Compliance
- SOC 2 Type II certified
- ISO 27001 compliant
- Cloud-hosted with enterprise-grade security
- Role-based access controls
- Data encryption in transit and at rest
